human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for automotive service-lane specificity, recall and quality-hold boundaries, traceability and supplier-escalation discipline, completion-evidence controls, commercialization notes, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to automotive dealer service-lane, warranty, campaign, parts-traceability, quality-hold, and completion-evidence work rather than generic service advising.

Authority sources cover FTC warranty and auto-repair guidance, NHTSA recall baselines, OEM public warranty and recall workflow references, IATF customer-specific requirements, and AIAG logistics and traceability references with refresh discipline.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, incomplete prerequisites, unsupported completion, traceability conflict, quality-hold and release-boundary handling, supplier substitution pressure, rework, adjacent-lane rejection, realistic terminology, low-confidence escalation, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant-specific adaptation.
Manifest and deployment notes keep runtime strategy as specification-based, document validated packaging, and define promotion criteria for any future retained implementation.

review_notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguities remain in the research-contract coverage.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around legal interpretation, goodwill approval, supplier substitution approval, recall release, and premature completion.

notes: This pack is suitable for validated deployment as an automotive service-lane spec pack and does not claim retained implementation authority.
Promotion to a retained implementation must be explicit and separate.
Functionality coverage is complete in both the evaluation results and the functional audit record.
